seoTitle: Frankston North vs Freeburgh — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest

ogTitle: Frankston North vs Freeburgh: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Frankston North and Freeburgh.
heading: Frankston North vs Freeburgh — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Frankston North, Victoria and Freeburgh, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Freeburgh has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Frankston North (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Frankston North or Freeburgh safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Frankston North has a flood risk score of 56/100, while Freeburgh scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Frankston North scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Freeburgh scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Frankston North or Freeburgh?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Freeburgh has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.98% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
